tier_remaining_note: These four fields drift from the live generate_build_plan_v2.py scoring model (a fresh recount on 2026-07-13 found A=12, B=746, C=7171, D=24631 remaining) and are not covered by verify_build_state.py's disk reconciliation, only route_pairs/blog/total_markdown_files are. tier_b_remaining above is only decremented by this chunk's 25 routes from its prior stored value, not recomputed from scratch. A future maintenance pass should add tier-remaining reconciliation to verify_build_state.py so these four fields stop drifting silently.
notes: Chunk 84 DONE (Template D, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 12 parallel research agents, one per country. Closed the Australia/UAE/Canada reciprocal gap for the 12-country Tier B cluster opened by the UK (chunk 80), US (chunk 81) and partial Canada/UAE (chunks 82-83) runs: Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine and Venezuela. Australia cluster (24 routes): all 12 countries, both directions, since none had any Australia coverage before this chunk. UAE cluster (14 routes): the 7 countries not already covered in chunks 82-83 (Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Russia, Serbia, Venezuela), both directions. Canada cluster (12 routes): the 6 countries not already covered in chunks 82-83 and with a workable Canada connection researched this run (Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Russia, Serbia), both directions; Canada-Venezuela deliberately deferred to keep the batch at 50. Genuine differentiators found: most of these 12 countries are DAFF non-approved for Australian import (no compliant direct path exists), forcing a 180-consecutive-day residency stopover in an approved country (commonly the US or UK, since American/Avianca/Delta already fly several of these origins into a CDC-registered or otherwise workable US gateway) before Australia's own RNATT titre test, wait and Mickleham quarantine even begin; route_complexity 'very_high' with 40-52 week timelines on non-approved-origin pages, matching the precedent set for Laos/Uzbekistan in chunk 78. Fiji was the standout exception: independently verified as DAFF-approved (Group 2, same bracket as Japan/Singapore, no titre test, 10-day minimum Mickleham quarantine) and MOCCAE low-risk-listed (no titre test), while still carrying its own mandatory BAF entry quarantine (7 days for Australia/NZ-origin pets under BAF's Group A tier, 30 days for UAE/Canada-origin pets under Group B) regardless of how lightly Australia or the UAE treat a Fiji-origin pet leaving in the other direction, a genuine asymmetry the Fiji agent verified rather than assuming a blanket 30-day rule as the brief initially suggested. Serbia is DAFF Group 3 approved (no 180-day residency needed) despite Air Serbia excluding pets from its Belgrade-Dubai and Belgrade-UK routes under its own carrier policy (confirmed consistent with the chunk 80/81 finding), forcing an alternative routing via Frankfurt or Vienna into the UAE specifically. Russia and Ukraine both required fresh verification of airspace/carrier restrictions specific to Australia/UAE/Canada rather than reusing the UK/US framing verbatim: a Dubai-Moscow direct corridor reopened via Aeroflot and FlyDubai in mid-2026 after a partial ceasefire, giving Russia-UAE the fastest routing in the whole batch, while Ukraine's civilian airspace remains closed and every Ukraine page routes overland to Poland first, with the Polish stopover doubling as the DAFF-approved-country residency clock where relevant. Guatemala, Honduras and El Salvador each carry a New World Screwworm-affected-country certificate requirement layered on their existing CDC-pathway facts, flagged as unconfirmed for the Australia/UAE/Canada legs specifically rather than assumed to carry over from the US-side pages. Venezuela's Caracas airport earthquake closure (24 June 2026) was independently re-verified as still in effect for this chunk rather than copied forward from the chunk 80/81 pages, with Avianca/Copa's Valencia-airport contingency routing carried into the new Australia/UAE pages and flagged for reconfirmation closer to travel. Bolivia surfaced a genuine diplomatic-access gap: no working Bolivian consulate in Australia (Sydney honorary consulate closed) and conflicting public sources on UAE representation, both stated honestly as open questions rather than guessed. QA pass after the agents finished caught and fixed one systematic issue the agents' own self-checks used inconsistent terminology for: several files used 'moderate' or 'extreme' instead of the schema's valid route_complexity values (medium/very_high), corrected across all affected files with a scoped fix touching only this chunk's 50 new files (a first attempt accidentally matched the same string across the entire existing 6,521-file corpus via an unscoped shell glob, caught immediately from the git diff stat before committing and fully reverted with zero impact to any pre-existing page). All 50 files QA-scanned clean after that fix (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary after manual review of 'embark'/'foster' substring hits, all legitimate: American Airlines' named PetEmbark cargo programme and 'fostering' in CFIA's own adoption-related commercial-dog definition; correct Marcus Webb persona on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser; all route_data fields populated; every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, zero broken links; all titles/descriptions/slugs confirmed unique across the batch; opening sentences independently varied and fact-led, no templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton on any of the 50). Routes 6,521 -> 6,571, pages 7,327 -> 7,377. Chunk 85 (Template E, Tier B) is next.
_previous_notes_chunk_82_83: Chunks 82-83 DONE (Template B + Template C, Tier B, 50 new routes, 2 blocks, one batch). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Closed reciprocal-pair gaps across three hub clusters: CANADA cluster (CFIA rules) with Brunei, Uzbekistan, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Tunisia and Ukraine (canada-to-brunei was the sole missing direction after brunei-to-canada shipped in chunk 81; uzbekistan, honduras, nicaragua, paraguay, tunisia and ukraine each got both directions with Canada); UAE cluster (MOCCAE rules) with Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Tunisia, Ukraine, Barbados, Guyana, Namibia and Zambia (both directions each); UK cluster (DEFRA/APHA rules) with Barbados, Guyana, Namibia, Zambia, Albania and Algeria (both directions each) plus Andorra and Bahamas (both directions) and a single Belarus-to-UK direction (UK-to-Belarus intentionally left for a future chunk); and a standalone France-Macau pair (both directions). Barbados, Guyana, Namibia, Zambia, Albania, Algeria, Andorra and Belarus each received their first route coverage on the site (previously zero pages). Genuine differentiators found: Brunei imposes quarantine on Canadian-origin pets regardless of paperwork (14 days with a passing titre test, 30 days without) because Canada isn't on Brunei's short rabies-free list, and Royal Brunei Airlines doesn't serve North America at all, so routing runs via Cathay Pacific Cargo or Malaysia Airlines Cargo (Singapore Airlines Cargo explicitly excludes Brunei from its live-animal network). CFIA's high-risk-for-dog-rabies list (dated 13 March 2025) covers Uzbekistan, Honduras, Tunisia and Ukraine but not Nicaragua or Paraguay; the resulting CFIA ban (since 28 June/28 September 2022 depending on species date) applies to commercial dogs only (resale, adoption, fostering, breeding, show, research), never an owner-accompanied personal pet, a distinction confirmed and stated explicitly across every Canada-side high-risk-country page this batch. MOCCAE requires a FAVN/RNATT titre test for every one of these origin countries except confirmation was inconclusive for Barbados (flagged as needing direct confirmation per the YMYL guard rather than guessed), and MOCCAE imposes no fixed post-titre waiting period the way the EU/UK do, making several UAE-bound routes faster than their UK counterparts despite both needing a titre test. Ukraine's civilian airspace remains closed as of mid-2026 (confirmed against EASA's Conflict Zone Information Bulletin and Ukraine's July 2026 Civil Aviation Development Strategy), so all four Ukraine pages route overland to Poland or Romania before any flight. Belarus is Part 2 listed by the UK (no titre test needed, same category as the US/Canada) but the EU/UK airspace ban on Belarusian carriers, imposed after the May 2021 Ryanair Flight 4978 forced landing and expanded again in December 2025, remains fully in force, so routing requires an overland transfer to Vilnius or Warsaw before flying. Andorra has no commercial airport, so both Andorra-UK pages route overland via Barcelona or Toulouse; Andorra is GB Part 1 listed (no titre test). Barbados is GB Part 2 listed and requires a Barbados-side non-stop Monday-to-Friday London flight per its own import conditions (APHA export certificate 3890), currently only satisfied by British Airways. Guyana is unlisted for GB entry but is one of only 11 countries Guyana's own GLDA authority treats as rabies-free for its own imports, including the UK; Guyana also sits on the CDC's high-risk list, which blocks American Airlines Cargo dog bookings originating there, ruling out a Miami/JFK connection for dogs (not cats) on Guyana-outbound routes. Namibia has no domestic FAVN lab, so titre blood samples route to the Onderstepoort Veterinary Institute in South Africa. Zambia has a named GB export certificate, EHC 3928 (version 3, January 2024 guidance), and a genuine nonstop Emirates Lusaka-Dubai service (EK713/EK714) despite no nonstop Lusaka-London route existing. Tunisia-UAE is one of the few genuinely direct routes on the site (Tunisair and Emirates both fly Tunis-Dubai nonstop); Tunisia's existing Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-type breed ban was confirmed as an import-side rule only, so it doesn't block those breeds leaving Tunisia for Canada. Macau-France found that Macau (unlike Hong Kong) is not on the EU's rabies-risk-exempt list under the Delegated Regulation (EU) 2026/131 that took effect 22 April 2026, so a Macau-origin pet needs the full titre test and 90-day wait into France despite Hong Kong next door being exempt; Macau-side facts were cross-checked against the already-published canada-to-macau.md and united-kingdom-to-macau.md pages for consistency before writing, no discrepancies found. All 50 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary after manual review of initial 'embark'/'foster' substring hits, which were all legitimate uses: American Airlines' PetEmbark cargo programme, animal 'fostering' in the specific adoption sense, and 'embarkation' as a genuine travel term, not the banned figurative usage; correct Marcus Webb persona on all 50; valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block verified with a Python parser; all route_data fields populated; every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, zero broken links found; opening sentences independently varied and fact-led, no templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton on any of the 50). Routes 6,471 -> 6,521, pages 7,277 -> 7,327. Chunk 84 (Template D, Tier B) is next. Content plan Day 32 (UK to South Africa route page) reconciliation check remains outstanding, not addressed this run.
_previous_notes_chunk_80: Chunk 80 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block). Also fixed a bookkeeping bug found at the start of this run: chunk 79 (Brunei/Laos/Seychelles/Trinidad and Tobago/Uzbekistan EU coverage + Macau UK/US/Australia gaps, routes 6,396 -> 6,421) was already committed to main (c890e545) with its route/page counts correctly bumped, but completed_chunks and next_chunk were left at 78/79 instead of advancing to 79/80, and the notes field was never rewritten to describe chunk 79's own content, only carried the stale chunk-78 narrative forward. Cross-checked against BUILD-PLAN.md (which correctly showed 'Chunks 1-79 complete, Chunk 80 next') and git log before treating chunk 80 as the correct next unit, rather than either re-building already-shipped chunk 79 content or skipping this run under the false premise that nothing was left to build. completed_chunks/next_chunk are now 80/81, correctly reflecting reality. Chunk 80 closed reciprocal UK gaps for 12 Tier B countries with no prior UK coverage in either direction: Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine and Venezuela (24 routes, both directions each) plus Bolivia-to-United-States (1 route) = 25 routes, researched via 12 parallel web-search agents, one per country. Genuine differentiators found: Russia and Fiji are in fact GB-listed (low-risk) for pet entry, contrary to the working assumption going into this chunk, so their UK-bound pages need no titre test or 3-month wait, only the standard 21-day post-vaccination wait; verified directly against gov.uk's primary listed-country pages rather than assumed from third-party aggregators. Russia-UK and Ukraine-UK both have no direct flight: Russian airspace has been closed to UK/EU aircraft and Russian carriers banned from UK/EU airspace since 2022 with no change confirmed for 2026, so Russia routing goes via Istanbul or Dubai; Ukrainian civilian airspace has been closed to all commercial flights since 24 February 2022 and remains closed as of July 2026 despite an EASA-tracked phased-reopening working group formed in March 2026, so Ukraine routing goes overland to Poland or Romania before flying. The UK's Ukraine Family Scheme / Homes for Ukraine pet provisions are still active in 2026 but only add a licence-application fallback path (up to 5 pets, possible home isolation instead of kennel quarantine), they do not relax the underlying titre-test/vaccination biosecurity requirements, a distinction both Ukraine pages state explicitly rather than implying a blanket exemption. Fiji's own import rules for pets arriving from the UK require a mandatory minimum 30-day quarantine at a BAF facility (Fiji does not treat the UK as low-risk even though the UK treats Fiji as low-risk), so quarantine_required is true only on the united-kingdom-to-fiji page, correctly asymmetric. Venezuela's international flight links remain genuinely volatile (Copa suspended Caracas flights over safety concerns; American Eagle cancelled seven flights in three days in July 2026), so the Venezuela pages recommend Avianca's Bogota-Heathrow connection as the most consistently available routing while stating plainly that availability should be reconfirmed close to travel rather than asserted as guaranteed. El Salvador is CDC high-risk for canine rabies, which has led American Airlines Cargo to embargo new dog cargo bookings from that origin (cats unaffected), a dog-specific complication unique to that route. Serbia's own Air Serbia flies Belgrade-Heathrow direct roughly ten times weekly but excludes all pet travel on UK, UAE and Qatar routes under its own policy, so the obvious direct flight is unusable and both Serbia pages route via Vienna or Frankfurt instead. Tunisia and Paraguay, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua all confirmed unlisted for GB entry (full titre-test pathway), each with a named export authority and a genuine local edge case (Tunisia's own breed ban on Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-crosses; Paraguay's 5-pet cap and separate breed ban; Guatemala's OIRSA USD 14 port-inspection fee; Nicaragua's non-physical 'quarantine permit' that is administrative paperwork, not confinement). All 25 files QA-scanned clean after a second manual pass caught and fixed three issues the agents' own self-checks missed: two files (paraguay-to-united-kingdom.md, united-kingdom-to-paraguay.md) had template_variant set to B and D respectively instead of this chunk's E, one broken link (a non-existent /pet-transport/countries/tunisia/ country guide, corrected to the UK origin hub), and one banned templated opening ('Moving a pet from the UK to Russia...') rewritten to lead with the specific fact instead. Routes 6,421 -> 6,446, pages 7,227 -> 7,252. Chunk 81 (Template A, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
_previous_notes_chunk_81: Chunk 81 DONE (Template A, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block). Built via 14 parallel research agents. Delivered the United States side of the reciprocal cluster opened in chunk 80: the same 12 countries (Bolivia, El Salvador, Fiji, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Russia, Serbia, Tunisia, Ukraine, Venezuela) each got united-states-to-X.md (12 routes); 11 of the 12 (all but Bolivia, since bolivia-to-united-states.md already existed) also got X-to-united-states.md (11 routes) = 23 routes, plus canada-to-maldives.md and brunei-to-canada.md (2 routes, genuinely new pairs, both directions previously unbuilt) = 25 routes. CDC high-risk-for-dog-rabies status independently verified per country against cdc.gov/importation/dogs/high-risk-countries.html (list dated 15 April 2026): El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Tunisia, Russia, Ukraine and Venezuela are HIGH RISK (FAVN titre test, CDC Dog Import Form, entry restricted to one of only six CDC-registered animal care facility airports: Atlanta, JFK, LAX, Miami, Philadelphia, Washington Dulles); Fiji, Nicaragua, Paraguay and Serbia are NOT high risk (standard CDC Dog Import Form only, no titre test), correcting a prior working assumption that Paraguay would be high risk. Genuine differentiators found: Fiji has a real asymmetry, no US-side quarantine or titre leaving Fiji, but a mandatory minimum 30-day BAF quarantine at Koronivia for a US-origin pet entering Fiji, since that is Fiji's own domestic biosecurity rule and applies regardless of origin. Russia and Ukraine both have no direct US flights for reasons distinct from their EU/UK routing problems: the FAA/US DOT blocked Russian aircraft from US airspace on 2 March 2022 (a separate US legal order from the EU/UK sanctions used on the chunk 80 pages), routed via Istanbul, Dubai or Doha; Ukraine's closed civilian airspace (since 24 February 2022, still closed per EASA's Conflict Zone Information Bulletin as of mid-2026) means a flight only reaches Warsaw, Krakow, Rzeszow or a Romanian city, with the final leg into Ukraine done overland by car. Venezuela's flight situation changed materially since chunk 80's UK pages were written: twin magnitude ~7.1-7.5 earthquakes struck northern Venezuela on 24 June 2026, structurally damaging Simon Bolivar International Airport (Caracas) and closing it to scheduled commercial traffic; airlines have rerouted through Valencia's Arturo Michelena airport instead (Avianca, Copa, Iberia, Air Europa), and American Airlines had to suspend its just-resumed (April 2026) nonstop Miami-Caracas service again through at least 10 July 2026 with no confirmed resumption date, superseding the older 'American Eagle cancellations' narrative used on the Venezuela-UK pages. El Salvador: found a real CDC-compliant workaround to the American Airlines dog cargo embargo established in chunk 80, Avianca and Delta both fly nonstop from San Salvador directly into CDC-registered facility airports; also confirmed USDA APHIS eFile endorsement is explicitly NOT required for pets exported from the US to El Salvador, an exception to the standard ~USD 38 pattern used on every other US-origin page. Guatemala: dogs also need a New World Screwworm-affected-country certificate (2026 APHIS/CDC NWS designation, alongside Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama) confirming a screwworm-free inspection within 5 days of shipment, layered on top of the CDC titre pathway; a MAGA import permit must be secured before a US-origin pet leaves. Honduras: United Airlines flies both San Pedro Sula and Palmerola nonstop to Houston, but Houston has no CDC-registered animal care facility, so that specific routing fails CDC entry rules for a dog even though the flight itself is real and convenient; American (Miami) and Delta (Atlanta) work instead. Nicaragua: the US export certificate must be ink-signed, embossed and mailed by USDA rather than endorsed electronically via the standard APHIS eFile system. Serbia: Air Serbia flies Belgrade-JFK direct and, unlike its blanket UK/UAE/Qatar pet exclusion, does carry pets cabin-only on this specific route (8kg cap, roughly EUR 130); could not confirm whether the US sits on Serbia's own favourable-risk import tier, stated as an open question per the YMYL guard rather than guessed. Tunisia: confirmed CDC high-risk, and the existing Pit Bull/Rottweiler/Tosa/Mastiff-type breed ban applies regardless of a pet's US origin. Bolivia: found the Bolivian Consulate in Washington DC requires an accompanying USD 85 money order, not a card payment, to legalise the export certificate. Canada-to-Maldives: paired Canada's CFIA export process (accredited vet plus CFIA endorsement, roughly CAD 20-40) with the Maldives' established dog-import ban and cats-only pathway, routed via Doha, Dubai or Abu Dhabi from Toronto. Brunei-to-Canada (a genuinely new pair, previously unbuilt in either direction): confirmed Royal Brunei Airlines carries no unaccompanied pet cargo on any route, so routing transits Singapore or Hong Kong; confirmed Brunei is not on CFIA's high-risk list, so no titre test applies on the Canada import side, a lighter pathway than both the CDC-titre US sibling route and the UK's titre-plus-90-day-wait route. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, correct Marcus Webb persona, valid keyed-map YAML for every requirements block, all route_data fields populated, no placeholder strings, no contact email or phone stated beyond legitimately-sourced government agency contacts already established on sibling pages, every sideways/upward link verified against real files on disk with a Python script, opening sentences independently varied and fact-led rather than a templated 'Moving a pet from X to Y' skeleton). Routes 6,446 -> 6,471, pages 7,252 -> 7,277. Chunk 82 (Template B, Tier B) is next. Content plan Day 32 (UK to South Africa route page) reconciliation check remains outstanding from chunk 80, not addressed this run.
